dimensionDescriptions.network_beaconing: Explain whether periodic timing patterns or low-volume callback traffic may indicate command-and-control activity and why these patterns are operationally meaningful.
dimensionDescriptions.network_exfiltration: Explain whether traffic volume, directionality, and destination characteristics suggest possible data staging or exfiltration behavior.
dimensionDescriptions.benign_traffic_explanations: Consider plausible legitimate explanations such as SaaS synchronization, automated backups, or system maintenance and explain why they may or may not fit the observed activity.
dimensionDescriptions.dns_patterns: Explain DNS characteristics such as query timing, subdomain structure, or TTL behavior and what these patterns might imply.
dimensionDescriptions.tls_metadata: Explain how TLS metadata such as certificate patterns or SNI behavior may support or weaken a given interpretation.
dimensionDescriptions.proxy_http_indicators: Explain what proxy or HTTP indicators (user-agent shifts, byte counts, request timing) may reveal about the nature of the activity.
dimensionDescriptions.endpoint_to_network_linkage: Explain how endpoint context such as process lineage, user activity, or scheduled jobs may relate to the network behavior.
dimensionDescriptions.investigation_pivot: Identify one or two investigative checks that could most quickly reduce uncertainty and explain why they would be informative.
dimensionDescriptions.containment_tradeoff: Explain the operational tradeoffs associated with a containment action, including potential business impact or loss of visibility.
dimensionDescriptions.risk_reasoning: Explain why the selected risk level follows logically from the potential impact and available evidence.
dimensionDescriptions.confidence_reasoning: Explain why the chosen confidence level reflects the strength and limitations of the available evidence.
dimensionDescriptions.evidence_linkage: Explain which specific artifacts support key conclusions and clearly distinguish evidence from inference.
dimensionDescriptions.hypothesis_alignment: Explain how the final assessment aligns with the reasoning process used during the simulation.

reportSections.title: Executive Summary
reportSections.template: Briefly summarize the most likely interpretation of the network activity, the associated risk, and the immediate recommendation.

reportSections.title: Traffic Pattern Interpretation
reportSections.template: Explain the dominant traffic pattern including timing behavior, directionality, and destination characteristics, and why it may suggest beaconing, exfiltration, or benign automation.

reportSections.title: Key Evidence
reportSections.template: Identify several important artifacts and explain how each supports or weakens the current interpretation.

reportSections.title: Hypothesis and Alternative Interpretations
reportSections.template: Explain the leading hypothesis and briefly consider at least one alternative explanation.

reportSections.title: Confidence and Remaining Uncertainty
reportSections.template: Explain the confidence level and identify remaining unknowns that could affect the assessment.

reportSections.title: Next Investigative Steps
reportSections.template: Describe one or two investigative actions that could most effectively confirm or challenge the current interpretation.

reportSections.title: Containment Considerations
reportSections.template: Discuss possible containment actions and the operational tradeoffs associated with each approach.

reportSections.title: Professional Conclusion
reportSections.template: Provide a short operational recommendation and outline the next monitoring or investigation steps.

gapRules.prompt: Consider identifying one investigative check that could most quickly clarify whether the activity represents beaconing, exfiltration, or legitimate automation.
You may want to connect at least one endpoint activity (process, user action, or scheduled task) to the observed network behavior.
Consider discussing one operational tradeoff associated with the recommended containment approach.
Explain why the selected confidence level appropriately reflects both the evidence available and remaining uncertainty.
